meta.name: The Olive Green Design System
meta.purpose: Comprehensive design guide for a premium, provenance-led olive oil brand. This system prioritizes craft, restraint, and brand coherence over generic UI patterns.
meta.note: ALWAYS reference this file when building features. This system reflects a confident, provenance-led brand with longevity. If it isn't found in an olive grove, it doesn't belong in the palette.
meta.brandPositioning: Premium olive oil brand - quietly crafted, exceptionally pure. Rooted in nature, guided by the grove.

brandVoice.philosophy: Craft-centric, not design-centric. Provenance-led, not generic-modern.

tone.primary: Confident restraint. One strong sentence is better than three explanatory ones.

contentDensity.rule: One strong sentence is better than three explanatory ones.
guidelines: Let imagery and space tell the story

good: The harvest, captured at its peak
avoid: We focus on growing grapes and ethical production...

designPhilosophy.overview: A confident, provenance-led olive oil brand with longevity. The system balances editorial sophistication with warmth, reflecting connection to the grove and craft.

restraint.description: Less is more. Restraint signals confidence.
restraint.manifestation: Limited color palette, minimal UI elements, generous white space, fewer button variants

provenance.description: Rooted in the grove. Every element should feel connected to olive oil production.
provenance.manifestation: Color palette from grove (olive, earth, harvest gold), photography of groves and harvest, craft-focused language

editorial.manifestation: Sentence case headings, generous line-height for reading, editorial card styles, photography-first layouts

craft.manifestation: Language focuses on making, harvesting, tradition. Visuals show process, place, people.

breathingRoom.description: Generous spacing prevents crowding and allows content to breathe.
breathingRoom.manifestation: Ample padding, comfortable line heights (1.6-1.7), space between elements, section separation

colors.rule: If it isn't found in an olive grove, it doesn't belong in the palette.
colors.status: LOCKED - These colors are final and should not be changed without design system review.

oliveGreen.usage: Primary brand anchor. 90% usage. Headers, navigation, key UI elements.
oliveGreen.contrast: Use with warm-off-white or white text

warmOffWhite.usage: Main background color. Replaces generic white. Creates warmth and premium feel.
warmOffWhite.contrast: Use with soft-charcoal or deep-olive text

softCharcoal.usage: Primary text color on light backgrounds. Softer than pure black.

deepOlive.usage: Alternative text color, deeper sections. Darker than olive-green.

mutedGold.usage: Sparingly used accent. ≤10% usage. Logo, subtle highlights only.

white.usage: Text on dark backgrounds (olive green, deep olive). Not for backgrounds.
white.contrast: High contrast on olive green and deep olive

lightBlue.reason: Not found in olive grove. Off-brand.

deepPurple.reason: Not found in olive grove. Off-brand.

typography.philosophy: Editorial sophistication. Sentence case over all caps. Generous line-height for long-form storytelling.

modern.family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Inter, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif

sans.usage: All other text - body, headings, navigation, UI elements.

logo.usage: Brand name display - 'THE' smaller (text-xs), 'OLIVE GREEN' larger (text-3xl), stacked vertically.
logo.letterSpacing: wider tracking for 'THE' (tracking-wider), tighter tracking for 'OLIVE GREEN' (tracking-tight)

hero.tailwindClasses: text-white text-5xl md:text-6xl font-bold leading-tight tracking-tight
hero.usage: Main hero heading - editorial, impactful. Use sentence case, not all caps.

examples.good: The harvest, captured at its peak

sectionHeading.tailwindClasses: text-soft-charcoal text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold font-sans mb-lg tracking-tight
sectionHeading.usage: Section headings. Editorial tone. Sentence case preferred.

eyebrow.tailwindClasses: text-muted-gold text-xs uppercase tracking-wider font-sans mb-sm
eyebrow.usage: Micro-heading above sections. Small caps or subtle tracking. Used for: 'From the Grove', 'Harvest 2025', etc.

body.usage: Paragraph text, descriptions, long-form storytelling. Optimized for reading.
body.size: text-base on mobile, text-lg on desktop for better readability

navigation.usage: Navigation links. Consider sentence case or small caps instead of all caps for warmth.
navigation.case: sentence case or small caps (not all caps)

small.usage: Secondary information, harvest notes, tasting profiles, fine print

spacing.principle: Generous spacing creates breathing room and emphasizes content hierarchy. Use consistent spacing units throughout.

application.sections: No gaps between major sections - seamless flow (0px)
application.components: Generous padding within sections (py-4xl px-xl for content sections)
application.text: Comfortable line heights (leading-relaxed, 1.6-1.7) and paragraph spacing (space-y-md)

Logo.description: Brand logo component with stacked text
Logo.usage: Use in Header component. Do not modify styling.

Header.description: Full-width navigation bar with olive green background

exactImplementation.loginButton: text-white text-sm md:text-base font-sans tracking-wide hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity

structure.announcement: Above main navigation - small white text, centered (optional)
structure.userActions: Far right - LOGIN button and CartIcon

Header.usage: Use at top of every page. Contains Logo, Navigation, and user actions.

Navigation.description: Navigation links component - considered, not generic

exactImplementation.link: text-white text-sm md:text-base font-sans tracking-wide hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity
exactImplementation.activeLink: text-white text-sm md:text-base font-sans tracking-wide opacity-100 underline transition-opacity

Navigation.case: Sentence case (not all caps) for warmth
Navigation.usage: Use in Header. Restraint signals confidence - fewer items preferred.

Hero.description: Large, impactful section with background image/gradient and text overlay

exactImplementation.container: relative w-full min-h-[600px] flex items-center justify-center overflow-hidden
exactImplementation.contentContainer: relative z-10 w-full max-w-7xl mx-auto px-xl py-4xl
exactImplementation.heading: text-white text-5xl md:text-6xl font-bold leading-tight tracking-tight mb-lg
exactImplementation.supportingText: text-white text-lg md:text-xl leading-relaxed space-y-md max-w-xl
exactImplementation.diagonalElement: absolute right-0 top-0 w-32 h-full bg-white/5 transform rotate-12 origin-top

Hero.usage: Use for hero sections. Heading should be editorial, not all caps.

ContentSection.description: Editorial section with warm background and text content

exactImplementation.heading: text-soft-charcoal text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold font-sans mb-lg tracking-tight
exactImplementation.content: text-soft-charcoal text-base md:text-lg font-sans leading-relaxed space-y-md

ContentSection.usage: Use for content sections. Warm-off-white background creates premium feel.

Button.description: Button component - reduced to 2 variants only
Button.philosophy: Restraint signals confidence. Only 2 button styles.

exactImplementation.baseClasses: px-xl py-md font-sans text-base tracking-wide transition-all duration-200

secondary.classes: bg-transparent text-olive-green border-2 border-olive-green hover:bg-olive-green hover:text-white
secondary.usage: Learn more, Our story - secondary actions

removed.primaryAccent: Too many variants create decision fatigue

hover.philosophy: Subtle darkening or desaturation. No scaling, no animation gimmicks.

Button.usage: Use for all interactive buttons. Only 2 variants - primary and secondary.

Card.description: Editorial card component - editorial blocks, not UI components
Card.philosophy: Editorial sophistication over UI components. Spacing and background contrast over borders.

removed.borders: Replaced with spacing and background contrast

Card.usage: Use for displaying content. Prefer soft background shifts (warm-off-white → white) over borders.

Icon.philosophy: Use less, or make them meaningful. Keep functional icons minimal and invisible.
Icon.usage: Keep functional icons minimal. Introduce brand icons for meaningful expression.

CartIcon.description: Shopping cart icon with prominent count badge

props.count: number (default: 0) - number of items in cart

exactImplementation.icon: w-6 h-6 text-white transition-all (scale-110 when hasItems)
exactImplementation.badge: absolute -top-2 -right-2 bg-muted-gold text-olive-green text-sm font-bold rounded-full min-w-[24px] h-6 px-2 flex items-center justify-center shadow-lg border-2 border-white
exactImplementation.badgeBehavior: Shows count, displays '99+' for counts over 99, white border for high contrast against olive green background, shadow for depth

visualStates.hasItems: Icon slightly larger (scale-110), prominent badge with white border and shadow for maximum visibility

CartIcon.usage: Use in Header for shopping cart. Badge is highly visible when count > 0 with white border, shadow, and larger size for clear indication of cart contents.

TextBlock.description: Typography component with heading and text

headingSizes.eyebrow: text-muted-gold text-xs uppercase tracking-wider font-sans mb-sm
headingSizes.section: text-soft-charcoal text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold font-sans mb-lg tracking-tight
headingSizes.hero: text-white text-5xl md:text-6xl font-bold leading-tight tracking-tight

TextBlock.usage: Use for text blocks. Includes new 'eyebrow' tier for micro-headings.

style.principle: Very important for olive oil brand. Photography should tell the story.

usage.hero: Full-width, impactful. Natural elements, grove scenes.
usage.backgrounds: Subtle, with overlay for text readability.

layout.principle: Full-width sections stacked vertically. Each section is a distinct visual block with its own background color.

content.alignment: left: heading, right: body text (split) or centered

transitions.principle: Smooth, subtle transitions. No animation gimmicks.

overlays.images: Ensure text contrast - white text on dark images

responsive.principle: Design adapts gracefully across screen sizes

breakpoints.mobile: Stack navigation, adjust text sizes, full-width sections maintained
breakpoints.tablet: Adjust column layouts, maintain spacing principles (md: prefix)
breakpoints.desktop: Full layout as designed, optimal viewing experience

always: Reference this file when building any feature
Use exact Tailwind classes specified in component implementations
Follow the component props API exactly
Use colors from the core palette only (olive-green, warm-off-white, soft-charcoal, deep-olive, muted-gold)
Follow typography scale for text sizing
Apply design principles: restraint, provenance, editorial, craft
Use sentence case for headings (not all caps)
Prefer brevity: one strong sentence over three explanatory ones
Remember: If it isn't found in an olive grove, it doesn't belong in the palette
never: Create custom colors outside the palette
Use arbitrary values for spacing (use scale: xs, sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl, 4xl)
Modify component implementations without updating this file
Use font-modern for anything except the logo
Create new components without documenting them here first
Use all caps for headings (use sentence case)
Use blue or purple in customer-facing UI

colorApplication.oliveGreen: Headers, navigation bars, primary backgrounds, key UI elements. 90% usage. Always with white or warm-off-white text.
colorApplication.warmOffWhite: Main background color. Replaces generic white. Creates warmth and premium feel.
colorApplication.softCharcoal: Primary text color on light backgrounds. Softer than pure black.
colorApplication.deepOlive: Alternative text color, deeper sections. Darker than olive-green.
colorApplication.mutedGold: Logo and sparing highlights only. ≤10% usage. Creates warmth without being too bright.

typographyApplication.modern: ONLY for Logo component. Never use elsewhere.
typographyApplication.sans: All other text - navigation, headings, body, UI elements.
typographyApplication.case: Sentence case for headings. Uppercase only for navigation (optional) or eyebrow text.

syncInstructions.whenChanging: When making changes to this file:
steps: 3. Run: npm run generate:tailwind (if colors/spacing/fonts changed)
4. Update component files to match new specifications
6. Update any copy/language to match brand voice guidelines

scripts.sync: npm run sync:design-system - Syncs changes from design-system.json to codebase
scripts.validate: npm run validate:design-system - Validates codebase matches design-system.json
scripts.generate: npm run generate:tailwind - Generates Tailwind config from design-system.json
